selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 0.6% and 20-day momentum is 43.1%.
Trend quality is supported by 50% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 17.0% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 4.5%, downside volatility near 1.5%, and a recent max drawdown of 6.1%.
Volume confirmation is -6.0% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is -0.5% versus SPY over 5 days and -0.3% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.69, confidence 0.43, and 0.3 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.84, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 2 articles across 2 sources, 0.3 effective weighted articles, average relevance 1.00, average source quality 0.78, confidence 0.79.
Broad market news leaned bullish for the Technology sector with score 0.68 and confidence 0.43.

Three large private-credit firms publicly reassuring investors about AI-related risks to their software borrowers reduces perceived tail risk for those borrowers. First-order transmission: reassurance likely narrows credit spreads and eases short-term funding/liquidity concerns for mid-market software firms dependent on private credit, lowering the risk of defaults or distressed asset sales. Second-order effects: calmer funding conditions support software companies’ near-term cashflow, capex and M&A plans and reduces sector volatility and downside pressure on technology valuations. Impact is broad across technology/software exposure rather than company-specific, so beneficiaries are sector-level (technology). The short-horizon effect is modest and transitory (limited magnitude) but positive for sentiment and risk appetite in tech over the next 1–2 weeks.
Sector overlay was trimmed by 45% because it overlaps with company or macro themes.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is -0.018 with low confidence.

news_evidence.title: Texas Instruments: The Backbone Of The Data Center Revolution (NASDAQ:TXN)
news_evidence.llm_reason: Seeking Alpha discusses TXN-specific initiatives (facility expansion, an acquisition into power market) which are directly relevant to the company. The content supports a positive structural thesis but appears analytical/opinion-based and likely has moderate rather than immediate impact on price in the 1–10 day horizon unless tied to a separate press release or earnings surprise.
